column16: The Digital Platform Modernization (DPM) Programme is a foundational component of Immigration Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC)âs broader modernization efforts. DPM is a multi-year investment that is being delivered through a phased implementation approach. DPM is delivering transformation across three consecutive and overlapping phases. The DPM Programme is divided into four tranches, with tranche 1 implementing a Client Experience Platform (CXP), tranche 2 being the initial implementation of the Case Management Platform (CMP), and tranches 3 and 4 adding additional lines of business. Tranches 1 and 2 must occur concurrently to allow the Department to transition from the legacy platform to a modern platform capable of meeting the growing demands placed on the Department and to respond to crises, emergencies and operational pressures effectively and nimbly. This task authorization is for the purchase of Enterprise Data Platform (EDP) licenses.

column16: The objective of this construction project is to redevelop Canadaâs unused property in Colombo, Sri Lanka to accommodate the Chancery of the High Commission of Canada in Sri Lanka, in Colombo. The 4,100 sq.m property is located at No.6 Gregoryâs Road in the Cinnamon Gardens area of Colombo and has been designed as a highly specialized facility requiring a high level of technical expertise to construct. The program requirements for the new Chancery will include, but are not limited to, a two storey addition of 2,000 sq,m. to an existing two storey 500 sq.m, heritage building with major upgrades to the existing building for a total building gross area of 2,500 sq. m. column15: ECCC requires a Contractor to conduct two (2) aerological observations per day, each day per year, as well as carry out management and operations duties, at the Fort Nelson Upper Air Station in British Columbia. The contractor will: fill and prepare helium balloons; release them for data collection; transmit the data; and adhere to the Manual of Upper Air Observations at all times. Additional duties include: notifying air traffic control when balloons are released; maintaining a supply inventory and ordering helium; preparing monthly reports; shipping and receiving program related items; performing snow removal as required and providing technical support and equipment maintenance.
